transcript: Welcome to Lesson 6.7 where I teach you how to add the SMS layers on your email and marketing automations. From the previous lessons, I taught you how to set up your emails, how to create the email templates, and how to generally create an email marketing campaign. And on the previous workshop as well, I taught you how to create a simple automation kung saan linatag natin lahat ng mga email templates na ginawa natin. And there's one more thing missing. We are missing the SMS layer. And that is what I'm exactly going to teach you today. So let's dive in to GoHighLevel. before you can even start sending SMS or messages in GoHighLevel the first thing that you need to do is to actually create or purchase a phone number and in GoHighLevel there is like an onboarded system which we call LC phone system which was supposed to be Twilio before but now GoHighLevel is using its own integrated system for the phone numbers but somehow it's still 20. So it's like they're white labeling it. So here, for you to be able to access that, you need to go to settings. And then you click on phone system. Okay. And as you can see here, there are no phone numbers yet. So the first thing that you're going to do is to click add a phone number and then click add phone number. And here you can select the phone numbers that you want to purchase. As you can see, the phone numbers in the United States are quite cheap. They're just $1.15 and some are $2.15. Now, let me just tell you what's the main difference of a local phone number and a toll-free number. Now, for local phone numbers, normally these are like for local states, right? And it feels more personalized compared to a toll-free number, which has a wider reach, but more like a generic phone number. So I would suggest that when you run campaigns with your customers, use local phone numbers, especially if you are in the United States, in Canada, in Australia, or in the UK. Now for this demo, we're not going to buy a phone number. We can still set up the SMS messages even without a phone number. But in this screen, And this is where you can purchase those phone numbers once you have your clients. Okay, so let's just go ahead and close that out. Once you purchase a phone number, all the phone numbers will show up here. Whatever phone number you purchase will show up here. Now, it doesn't stop there. Before you can even start using that phone number, you need to register that phone number to A2P 10 DLC. fee. That's a regulatory set by the cell companies in the United States and network companies in the United States to avoid getting more spam for their subscribers. And before you can start sending, you need to do that. It's a non-negotiable and you cannot start sending without that or you're going to be fined heavily and you don't want that to happen because the fines for sending these unregulated or unsolicited text messages are going to be off the roof. Okay? So, don't send SMS messages if you haven't validated and registered it yet with A2P 10 DLC. So how do you do that How do you sign up or how do you validate with the A2P 10GLC Simple you just need to go to Trust Center here and then here you can see A2P Messaging This is a required registration to send business text messages through the U.S. carriers. Alright, so this is mainly for the U.S. okay now you just need to click on start registration and then you just need to follow through all the steps here as you can see there's what we call the profile needs the business details the business address the contact info business use case registration flow and everything in there you need to register that basically whatever message that you're going to send you have to register it okay so you have to register that so once you're done with this you just need to click on continue and that should take a few days before it gets verified or registered through a2p all right so you just have to wait for that you'll get notified in your account in in jhl if it's already been uh registered and verified with a2p and until then you cannot send any uh sms messages all right but if you already verified that part you already have like a2p 10 dlc especially for US clients and US businesses, you can now proceed in adding SMS in the account. All right. So I'm going to show you a few ways on how you can send SMS using GHL. But these are like, you know, like not the full automation. Later on, I'm going to show you the full automation where we add the SMS messages to the automation that we created from the previous workshop. but for now I'm going to show you how to send like one by one text messages okay so if you go to marketing here marketing and then click on snippets so here you can set up snippets like email snippets text snippets and basically for text or sms this is where you create the templates okay so click on new snippet and then create add text snippet right here you can select a snippet name so let's just call this like a welcome text all right and here let's just say hi in the air and then use their first name uh account name right welcome to fit mom ph to download your free guide, click this link. And then the link goes here. So basically, that's it. I strongly recommend not really putting attachments because it will not be an SMS. It's going to be an MMS or what we call the multimedia messaging. so do not attach any kind of uh do not attach any kind of images or any kind of attachments to and keep it simple as this so just plain text is fine all right so once you have created that template for your welcome text then you can just save it and you should be able to use it from this screen all right so you already have the template for that whenever you're going to send a welcome text you can use that and a few ways to use it is that if you go to contacts and then you select a contact let's use my contact here and then click this more button and set SMS as you can see here let's just create an action name it's basically for recording purposes and recording through the history so let's say send SMS send welcome SMS normally if you already have like a phone number you can just select the phone number from here or you can purchase a phone number from here But I suggest you don't do that because that will charge your account right now. I only suggest you doing this once you already have a client. But because even though you don't have a phone number right now, we can still proceed in setting up the automation. Although we cannot test it, but it's pretty much going to work the same way as the email. so no need to really purchase a phone number right now okay and here you can just simply select uh the sms template so this is the one that we created earlier so welcome text you select it and then you'll see here that it's already pasted like the sms message in here and then all you need to do is to just click on send sms so if you are sending to multiple contacts so you can either send all at once meaning if you select like 50 contacts if you select this option then it will automatically send 50 SMS to 50 contacts. Now, you can also send it at a scheduled time. Let's say, for example, you wanted to schedule this text message to go out tomorrow at 9 a.m., then you can select this and then select the date and time and then you can send that at a scheduled time. Now, the third option is actually a drip mode. A drip mode means you are going to control how many contacts are going to be receiving those messages in a specific time that you're going to identify, right? Let's say, for example, you only want to send 50 emails or 50 texts per hour, then you can set it up right here. So, as you can see, you can select the date and time. So, let's say it's going to start sending the messages during that time. You need to select how many messages do you want to send per batch. So let's say I want to send 10 messages per batch, and then I'm going to repeat that every two days, and then it's only going to be sent during weekdays. So this is what a drip campaign means. It's basically you are limiting the amount of messages that you send out so you don't get flagged as a spam, because normally if you don't do this, your account will get flagged and eventually your number might get blocked. So sending in drip mode is actually recommended if you're sending it to multiple or many contacts. All right. And then once everything has been set, you just click on send SMS and that should start sending your SMS. Now, this one is just red because basically we're just using this as an example. Okay. But that's how you send a message or an SMS through a non-workflow automation setup. Now, I'm going to teach you how to do it on an automation setup. So let's just go back to automation. And then we are going to go back to this automation workflow that we created from the previous workshop. Now, as you can see here, this is the FitMom PH lead capture to enrollment. This is the same template that you used where we created like the welcome. Sorry, this is the same automation that we used from the previous lesson wherein we created the framework. So we created a form submitted trigger and then we created the emails in here, the weight. So it's basically the same. All right. Now for you to add the SMS is very simple. Okay. As long as you have the templates, you can already add them in here. So it's a matter of like just when you send like where do you want to position the SMS? If you want an SMS message to be sent after the welcome email you can also do that So let say for example here we sending the welcome email You just click on this button and then find SMS and then click on send SMS All right From this point you can either just call it like welcome SMS. You can either select a template. So as you can see here, this is the template that we created earlier and we can definitely use it here. Okay. So you can see it right there and then we can just save that action. All right. So what this means is that when a person submits a form, they will receive a welcome email and then also they will receive a welcome SMS at the same time. Now, if you want to schedule like a different email, like you can also do that as long as you set it up properly here. Right. Now, as a rule of thumb, it's not really required to send an SMS together with an email. Normally, you send SMS as reminders. If they didn't convert, then if they didn't open the email, you can add the SMS in there. Let's say, for example, hey, Mr. Customer, you missed the 30-day money reset or you missed the document that we sent you, but no worries. Here's the link. You can download it directly. So that's how you can raise urgency using a text message because like what we've talked about in the lesson, there is a really high chance that your text will get opened around 90% because text, it may be a bit like old-fashioned, but people open their text messages. All right? So again, you can always position an SMS anywhere you want as long as you plan it out or you map it out in your funnel mapping and in your planning. So let's say, for example, I just want to add another text in here that will go out together with the nurture email 3 on day 6. So I'll just send an SMS. And then if I don't want to use a template, I can just simply create here, hi. And then let's use their first name, contact, first name. You missed the PDF download, but don't worry. here's the link to get it now and then put the link. Okay. www.link.com slash download. Alright. And then just save the action. And that's pretty much it. SMS is very simple to set up. Really, it's a lot simpler than setting it up with email. Now, the only work that needs to be done is really the A2P registration. if you have like if you are on a different country like the Philippines there's really the numbers are really expensive just so you are aware it's like $100 to $50 to $100 so it's really expensive so SMS really I would suggest if you are here in the Philippines and you want to try SMS marketing find a different solution that can do that because it's really expensive to do it in GoHighLevel unless you want to pay for that number and number from GHL then it's fine if you want to pay 5000 pesos that's fine so this lesson is actually really short it's as simple as that that's how you can set up the SMS messaging in GoHighLevel and I'll see you in the next lesson

The image shows a screenshot of a computer screen with a presentation slide or video frame. The key information shown in this presentation slide or video frame is:

**Libre Platform Screenshot**

* A screenshot of the Libre platform, specifically the **Snippets** page, which appears to be part of a marketing automation tool.
* The **Snippets** page has a green sidebar on the left with various menu options, including:
	+ Landing/Paid
	+ Dashboard
	+ Conversations
	+ Contacts
	+ Opportunities
	+ Payments
	+ Marketing
		- Automation
		- Sites
		- Memberships
		- Media Storage
		- Reporting
		- SEO
		- Apps & Integrations
* The main content area displays a table with the following columns:
	+ **Name**
	+ **Body**
	+ **Folder**
	+ **Type**
	+ **Date Updated**
* The table is currently empty, with a message indicating **No data available**.
* A **New Folder** and **New Snippet** button are located at the top right of the page.
* A small video window in the bottom-right corner shows a person, likely the presenter.

Overall, this screenshot appears to be demonstrating the Snippets feature within the Libre platform, which is likely used for creating and managing text snippets for marketing automation purposes.

The image shows a screenshot of a computer screen displaying a Libre phone system settings page. 

*   The top-left corner features a green sidebar with the "Libre" logo and various menu options, including "Settings," "My Staff," "Opportunities & Pipelines," "Calendars," "Email Services," "Phone System," "WhatsApp," "Objects," "Custom Fields," "Custom Values," "Manage Scoring," "Domains & SSL Redirects," and "External Tracking."
*   The main section of the page is titled "Phone System" and has several tabs, including "Phone numbers," "Regulatory Bundles," "Messaging," "Voice," "Trunk Cost Center," and "Additional Settings."
*   Below the tabs, there is a subheading that reads "Manage Numbers" and a search bar to manage phone numbers and their configurations.
*   A table displays the following columns: 
    *   **Numbers**
    *   **Friendly Name**
    *   **Forwarding Number**
    *   **Call Timeout**
*   The table currently has no data, indicated by a green circle with a lowercase "i" inside and the text "No data."
*   In the bottom-right corner, there is a small video window showing two people. 

The overall design of the page suggests that it is part of a larger system for managing phone numbers and configurations within an organization.

The image shows a screenshot of a computer screen displaying a Libre platform interface with a green sidebar on the left.

The green sidebar contains various menu options, including "Billing," "My Staff," "Opportunities & Pipelines," "Calendars," "Email Services," "Phone System," "Website," "Objects," "Custom Fields," "Custom Values," "Message Scoring," "Domains & URLs, Workflows," and "External Tracking." A white search bar is located at the top of the green sidebar.

The main section of the screen features a white background with a heading that reads "Phone System" and a subheading that says "Back to the phone center." Below this, there are several tabs: "Phone numbers," "Regulatory Bundles," "Messaging," "Voice," "Trunk Center," and "Additional Settings." The "Profile Needs" tab is currently selected.

Underneath the tabs, there is a section titled "Determine Profile Needs." This section includes a question about the country where the business entity is registered, with options for "US (United States)," "CA (Canada)," and "Other." A dropdown menu labeled "Business Details" contains fields for "Name and Address" and "Profile Needs." Another dropdown menu labeled "Brand Details" has a field for "Business Use Case." A third dropdown menu labeled "Campaign Details" includes fields for "Messaging Use Case," "Registration Flow," and "Other Details."

In the bottom-right corner of the screen, there is a small video window showing a man. The overall layout suggests that this is a screenshot from an instructional video or tutorial on setting up a phone system using the Libre platform.

The image shows a screenshot of a computer screen displaying a marketing automation platform, likely Libre, which is used for creating and managing marketing campaigns. The screenshot appears to be from an educational presentation or tutorial on marketing automation.

The image presents a screenshot of a computer screen displaying a Libre software interface, with a pop-up window for bulk SMS messaging. The pop-up window is titled "Bulk SMS" and features the following key elements:

* A text box at the top that reads, "Send Messages to Multiple Contacts"
* An option to attach files, with a maximum size limit of 5 MB per file
* A mode selection section with three options:
	+ Send All At Once
	+ Send at Scheduled Time
	+ Send in Drip Mode
* A calendar widget for selecting a date and time, with the current date set to March 26, 2025

The background of the image shows the Libre software interface, which includes various tabs and menus, such as:

In the bottom-right corner of the image, there is a small video window showing two people. The overall layout and design of the image suggest that it is a screenshot from an instructional video or tutorial on using the Libre software for bulk SMS messaging.

The image shows a screenshot of a computer screen displaying a workflow diagram for "FilMom PH Lead Capture to Enrollment."

The overall impression is that this is a screenshot of a workflow builder or automation tool, possibly used for marketing or customer engagement purposes.

The image shows a screenshot of a computer screen displaying a workflow diagram on a website. The workflow diagram is titled "F2F/Mon PH Lead Capture to Enrolment" and features a series of boxes connected by arrows, with each box containing a brief description.

*   **Video Call Window**

*   **Browser Toolbar**
    *   At the top of the screen, there is a browser toolbar with various icons and menus.

Overall, the image suggests that the user is working on designing a workflow diagram for a business process, possibly related to lead capture and enrollment. The presence of a video call window indicates that the user may be collaborating with others or receiving guidance on the process.
